description: Evaluation test scenarios for gate-lifecycle-integration skill

scenarios.name: Clean tool evaluation passes
scenarios.context: Code review of a new tool in src/tools/
scenarios.input_description: A tool that: is registered in plugin.ts, has Zod schema, calls SDK via session-api.ts wrappers, returns structured response via tool-response.ts, lives in src/tools/, has test file in tests/tools/, is under 200 LOC, has no any types

assertions.reason: Tool registered, factory pattern, Zod schema, structured response
Write-side only: calls SDK mutations, may write state, never reads events
Tool name matches plugin.ts key, proper signature, [Harness] errors
Lives in src/tools/, shared utilities in src/shared/, no state storage
Under 200 LOC, no inline business logic, test file exists

scenarios.name: CQRS-violating tool fails
scenarios.context: Code review of a tool that reads events directly
scenarios.input_description: A tool that subscribes to the event stream directly, bypassing hooks, and makes delegation decisions based on observed events

assertions.reason: AP-01 variant: tool reads event stream directly — events are read-side, tool must not observe them
Registration and factory pattern may still be correct

scenarios.required_remediation: Remove direct event stream access from tool. Use hook for event observation instead.

scenarios.name: Clean hook evaluation passes
scenarios.context: Integration check after hook factory modification
scenarios.input_description: A hook that: is registered via factory in src/hooks/, observes events only, has try/catch error boundary, extracts sessionID via getEventSessionID(), never calls patchSessionContinuity(), never calls delegationManager.dispatch()

assertions.reason: Factory pattern, dependency injection, event observer signature
Read-side only: observes events, no state mutations, no dispatch calls
try/catch present, silent failure, no unhandled rejections
Lives in src/hooks/, no cross-root imports
sessionID via getEventSessionID(), handles missing sessionID

scenarios.name: Write-side violating hook fails
scenarios.context: Code review of a hook that calls patchSessionContinuity
scenarios.input_description: A hook handler in src/hooks/ that calls patchSessionContinuity() to update delegation status when it observes session.idle events, without being the documented tool.execute.after exception

assertions.reason: AP-01: WRITE FROM READ-SIDE — hook calling patchSessionContinuity() outside documented exceptions
May still have proper error handling

scenarios.required_remediation: Move state mutation to a tool (write-side). Hook should only observe and trigger notification or routing.

scenarios.name: Lean plugin composition passes
scenarios.input_description: plugin.ts under 200 LOC: instantiates DelegationManager and LifecycleManager, wires hook factories, registers all tools, lazy-loads PTY, no inline business logic, delegates all logic to factories and lib modules

assertions.reason: All tools registered, all hooks wired, single-instantiation of managers
No inline business logic, PTY lazy-loaded, observers composed from factories
Correct dependency injection to all factory functions
Policy loaded once at startup, passed to components

scenarios.name: Fat plugin fails
scenarios.context: Phase audit of plugin.ts that accumulated inline logic
scenarios.input_description: plugin.ts with 350 LOC: inline delegation logic instead of using DelegationManager, hardcoded agent configs, synchronous PTY loading, event handler with business logic inline

assertions.reason: Over 200 LOC threshold, inline business logic
AP-14 variant: module over LOC limit with inline logic that belongs in lib/
Direct logic instead of factory delegation

scenarios.required_remediation: Extract inline logic to src/lib/ modules or src/tools/ factories. Reduce plugin.ts to pure composition under 200 LOC.

scenarios.name: Bounded delegation depth passes
scenarios.input_description: A delegation chain: root→child→grandchild (depth 3), valid category (research), queue key built via buildDelegationQueueKey(), dual-signal completion via CompletionDetector, grace period cleanup active, recovery guarantee derived from execution mode

assertions.reason: Uses delegationManager.dispatch(), no manual session creation
Valid category, proper queue key construction
Depth 3 = MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH, within budget
CompletionDetector with 3 stable polls, adaptive polling
Recovery guarantee set, grace period cleanup active

scenarios.name: Unbounded delegation depth fails
scenarios.input_description: A delegation chain: root→child→grandchild→great-grandchild (depth 4), exceeding MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH of 3. No depth check before dispatch. Category is 'analysis' which is not in VALID_DELEGATION_CATEGORIES.

assertions.reason: AP-06: UNBOUNDED DELEGATION DEPTH — depth 4 exceeds MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH (3)
AP-10: CATEGORY MISMATCH — 'analysis' not in VALID_DELEGATION_CATEGORIES

scenarios.required_remediation: Enforce depth check before dispatch. Change category to one of: research, implementation, review, visual-engineering, deep, quick.
